revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八神社]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| name = Hachi Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image = File:Hachi-jinja1.jpg
| location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Ginkakuji-chō 26
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.797194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, Ashihara-musubi, Ikumusubi, {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|lt=Koto-shiro-nushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumono|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}
| established = Either during the {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|qid=Q295082|lt=Daidō era|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} (806–810) or {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi era|1=Engi (era)}} (901–923)
| main_festival = Spring Festival (April 24), Autumn Festival (October 24)
| website = [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=650 Kyoto Tourism Navi]
| location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|qid=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}
}}

'''Hachi Shrine''' (八神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, within the {{ill|Ginkakuji|ja|銀閣寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ginkakuji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 慈照寺|1=Ginkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q257473}} district. The shrine previously served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} of the {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|en|Ginkaku-ji|qid=Q257473|lt=Jishō-ji|1=Ginkaku-ji}}.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is uncertain, with records suggesting either the {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|qid=Q295082|lt=Daidō era|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} (806–810) or the {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi era|1=Engi (era)}} (901–923). 

During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Hachi Shrine was associated with the nearby {{ill|Jōdo-ji|en|Jōdo-ji|qid=Q9016936|lt=Jōdo-ji|1=Jōdo-ji}} (currently {{ill|Jōdoin|ja|浄土院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdoin|1=Jōdo-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11556824}}) and was known by various names such as "Hassho Daimyōjin," "Jūzenshi Shrine," and "Juge Shrine." 

After the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}} caused significant damage to Jōdo-ji, the temple was relocated by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|en|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qid=Q447034|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimasa|1=Ashikaga Yoshimasa}} in the 14th year of the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era|1=Civilization}} (1482) to the west of the {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|qid=Q1786709|lt=Shōkoku-ji|1=Shōkoku-ji}}. The vacated land was transformed into Yoshimasa's Higashiyama Mountain Villa, which later became the temple {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|en|Ginkaku-ji|qid=Q257473|lt=Jishō-ji|1=Ginkaku-ji}}, making Hachi Shrine its {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}}.

== Deities ==
* Primary deities:
** {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}
** {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
** {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}
** Ashihara-musubi
** Ikumusubi
** {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}
** {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
** {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|lt=Koto-shiro-nushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* Subordinate deity:
** {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumono|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}

== Grounds ==
The shrine grounds include:
* Main Hall
* Middle Gate
* Worship Hall
* Juge Inari Shrine (dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
* Rain Shrine (dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Ogame no Kami|1=Kuraokami}})
* Shrine Office

== Festivals ==
* Spring Festival: April 24
* Autumn Festival: October 24

== References ==
* Historical records from the shrine
* Kyoto Tourism Navi entry on Hachi Shrine

== Related Articles ==
